name: Fig Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2-2 cups dried fruit (pineapple, golden raisins, peaches)
1/2-3/4 cup brown sugar (use 3/4 cup for best results)
Egg substitute equivalent to 2 eggs (use Eggbeaters or similar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the figs with boiling water and let stand about 10 minutes. Drain and chop.
2. Chop the other dried fruit in small pieces and combine with the chopped figs.
3. In a separate bowl, mix flour with baking soda and spices (cinnamon, ground cloves).
4. Combine 1/2 cup applesauce and brown sugar until smooth. Beat in egg substitute.
5. Add grated carrot and potato to the wet mixture. Gradually stir in flour mixture until smooth.
6. Pour batter into a greased 1 1/2-quart mold, filling about 2/3 full. Cover with lid or foil.
7. Place mold on a rack set over an inch of boiling water in a large pot. Steam for 2 hours, adding more boiling water if needed.
8. Remove from heat and serve hot.
Lemon Sauce:
1/4 cup sugar
1 tablespoon cornstarch
1 cup water
Grated rind of one lemon
1 1/2 tablespoons lemon juice
Combine sugar, cornstarch, and water in a double boiler over hot water until thickened. Remove from heat, stir in lemon rind and juice, and serve.
